preamble: The shift from the voiced alveolar fricative [z] to the voiced postalveolar fricative [ʒ] is a form of palatalization, often triggered by a following palatal glide or high front vowel.

examples.note: Dialectal palatalization of /z/ in syllable-final position (e.g., in Lisbon or Rio de Janeiro).

sources: Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
